edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: gpt-oss-120b generated all ~7M reasoning trajectories in Nemotron-Math-v2 under six configurations (high/medium/low reasoning x with/without Python TIR), 8 samples per configuration; these traces are the supervision signal for SFT of the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 chat.
description_variants: gpt-oss-120b generated all ~7M reasoning trajectories in Nemotron-Math-v2 under six configurations (high/medium/low reasoning x with/without Python TIR), 8 samples per configuration; these traces are the supervision signal for SFT of the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 chat.

gpt-oss-120b autoformalized natural-language theorems into Lean 4 statements via an iterative pipeline (initial formalization, Lean 4 compilation check, backtranslation/semantic verification, iterative refinement up to 8 iterations, faithfulness filtering); these formalizations train the Nemotron-3-Super BF16 chat through Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1.

GPT-OSS-120B generated the GPQA-style MCQA questions and reasoning traces and the RQA synthetic chemistry questions in Nemotron-Science-v1; these synthetic samples are the supervision signal for SFT of the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 chat.
GPT-OSS-120B is one of the frontier generators that produced synthetic chat and instruction-following dialogues (and corresponding responses) in Nemotron-Instruction-Following-Chat-v1.
GPT-OSS-120B synthetically generated the questions and agent skills that constitute the prompt side of Nemotron-SFT-OpenCode-v1.
GPT-OSS-120B is one of six frontier generators producing synthetic dialogues in Nemotron-Instruction-Following-Chat-v2.

Answer content in Nemotron-SpecializedDomains-Finance-v1 was synthetically generated by GPT-OSS-120B, which produced 5 candidate answers per question with different random seeds, grounded in the mapped SEC filing sections, prior to GenSelect filtering.
GPT-OSS-120B simulated multi-role agent interactions in the Interactive Agent subset of Nemotron-Agentic-v1.
GPT-OSS-120B in its low-reasoning mode (Du et al., 2025) generated the low-effort reasoning SFT samples (2% of the SFT data) covering math reasoning, STEM QA, and instruction following.

GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased ~565 SecQue templates into S&P-500/fiscal-year combinations and sampled five candidate answers per question to feed the GenSelect best-of-N answer selection — content for the financial reasoning SFT dataset.
GPT-OSS-120B generated CUDA kernels and reasoning traces alongside DeepSeek-R1 for the 100K-sample CUDA SFT dataset.
gpt-oss-120b played a role in the conversational tool-use SDG pipeline (one of six teacher models).
gpt-oss-120b generated tool-calling data alongside Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507.
gpt-oss-120b generated AoPS-style synthetic content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic Stack Exchange content.
gpt-oss-120b generated AoPS+SE math/proof content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic safety content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic STEM content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic chat data.
gpt-oss-120b generated tool-use interactive content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic math content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic Scale HLE content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic CDQuestions content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic GPQA-style content.
gpt-oss-120b generated synthetic Vedantu (educational platform) content.
gpt-oss-120b generated HelpSteer/LMSYS/Personas synthetic content.
gpt-oss-120b generated OpenMathReasoning-style synthetic content.
gpt-oss-120b generator.

CUDA SFT samples (100K) include kernel generation, repair, and optimization traces co-generated with DeepSeek-R1 and GPT-OSS-120B (validated via CUDA-execution evaluation). GPT-OSS-120B (low-effort mode) also generates the shorter reasoning traces forming 2% of the SFT data for the new Low-effort reasoning mode.
GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased SecQue-derived templates and produced GenSelect candidate answers for the 366k Financial Reasoning SFT pairs.
GPT-OSS-120B served as a generator in the CUDA SFT data pipeline alongside DeepSeek-R1.
gpt-oss-120b was used in the Conversational Tool Use 6-stage SFT pipeline.
GPT-OSS-120B operating in low-effort mode generated the low-effort reasoning samples (math, STEM QA, instruction following) that constitute 2% of Nemotron 3 Super's SFT data.
GPT-OSS-120B used as a CUDA SFT teacher (alongside DeepSeek-R1) and as the source of low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data) that train the low-effort reasoning mode.

GPT-OSS-120B is co-teacher (with DeepSeek-R1) for the CUDA SFT corpus and additionally generates the low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data, in low-effort mode) that train Super3's low-effort reasoning mode.
GPT-OSS-120B contributed CUDA kernel SFT samples and (via low-effort mode) shorter reasoning traces used in Nemotron 3 Super SFT.

GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased SecQue template instantiations into up to three reformulations per combination and produced five candidate answers per question with distinct seeds for the GenSelect best-of-N step.
GPT-OSS-120B generated CUDA C++ kernels, repair traces, and natural-language specifications for the 100K-sample CUDA SFT dataset.
GPT-OSS-120B was used in stages of the six-stage Conversational Tool Use SDG pipeline.
GPT-OSS-120B (in low-effort mode) generated training samples covering math reasoning, STEM QA, and instruction-following that introduce a low-effort reasoning mode into Nemotron 3 Super during SFT.
GPT-OSS-120B served as a teacher for two SFT subsets: CUDA kernel data (jointly with DeepSeek-R1) and the new low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data) collected from its low-effort mode.

Generator model openai/gpt-oss-120b produced (a portion of) the "Synthetic Chat from gpt-oss-120b, Mixtral-8x22B-Instruct-v0.1, Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507, and Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507" synthetic dataset used for BF16 post-training.

Generator model openai/gpt-oss-120b produced (a portion of) the "Synthetic Art of Problem Solving and Stack Exchange from gpt-oss-120b, Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct, and Goedel-Prover-V2-32B" synthetic dataset used for BF16 post-training.
Generator model openai/gpt-oss-120b produced (a portion of) the "Synthetic Conversational Agentic Tool-Use RL" synthetic dataset used for BF16 post-training.
GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased SecQue template instantiations and sampled five candidate answers per question for the Financial Reasoning SFT dataset of Super 120B-A12B Instruct.
GPT-OSS-120B was used in the synthetic CUDA data generation pipeline that produced ~100K kernel-generation/repair/optimization training samples for Super 120B-A12B Instruct.
gpt-oss-120b is one of six LLMs used across the six-stage conversational tool-use pipeline for Super 120B-A12B Instruct SFT.

GPT-OSS-120B operating in its low-effort mode generated the 2%-share of low-effort math/STEM-QA/instruction-following SFT samples that introduce the low-effort reasoning mode in Super 120B-A12B Instruct.
GPT-OSS-120B generated 5 candidate answers per question (with distinct random seeds) for the GenSelect strategy used in Financial Reasoning SFT.
GPT-OSS-120B generated CUDA C++ kernel candidates and reasoning chains for Super's CUDA SFT data.
GPT-OSS-120B used in the conversational tool-use synthetic data pipeline.
GPT-OSS-120B (low-effort mode) generated low-effort reasoning training samples (math, STEM QA, IF) used to introduce the low-effort reasoning mode in Super's SFT.
GPT-OSS-120B generated CUDA SFT data (kernel generation/repair/optimization) and produced shorter low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data) used to teach the low-effort reasoning mode.

SWE 2 RL rollouts launch isolated sandboxes built from SWE-Gym Docker images. Each rollout boots an SWE-Gym repository instance, runs the OpenHands agent loop to produce a code patch, then runs ground-truth tests for binary reward.
SWE-Gym issues + containerized execution environments are part of the Software-Engineering SFT data for Nemotron 3 Super.

SWE-Gym container images and tasks were converted to Apptainer .sif files and used as RL environments for the SWE-RL stages of Nemotron 3 Super (Stage 2.1 single-step tool use; Stage 2.2 OpenHands agent loop with binary patch reward).

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 was post-trained on SWE-Gym/SWE-Gym as a 18.82% mixing-ratio component of the SWE 2 stage blend (split `swe2`, 1,444 samples) within Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends.
Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B is RL-trained at the SWE 1 stage on a blend (swe1.jsonl split of nvidia/Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends) in which SWE-Gym/SWE-Gym contributes 20.30% of the mixture.

SWE-RL uses SWE-Gym training environments — Docker images converted to Apptainer .sif — to provide the repository-isolated sandbox in which the model produces patches scored by ground-truth tests.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B was trained on SWE-Gym/SWE-Gym as part of the SWE 2 RL training stage blend, with mixing ratio 18.82%. The blend (split 'swe2') contains 1,444 samples (1008M).

edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 served as a generator for the chat refresh and for instruction-following responses in Nemotron-Instruction-Following-Chat-v1.
description_variants: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 served as a generator for the chat refresh and for instruction-following responses in Nemotron-Instruction-Following-Chat-v1.
Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 is one of six frontier generators producing synthetic dialogues in Nemotron-Instruction-Following-Chat-v2.
Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 simulated multi-role (user/agent/tool-environment) interactions in the Interactive Agent and Tool Calling subsets of Nemotron-Agentic-v1.

Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 generated the synthetic snippets concatenated into long-context inputs for Super's long-context SFT dataset, plus the seven sequential reasoning tasks that train left-to-right context processing.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-4B-BF16
edges.description: BF16 was produced by compressing the parent NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 weights via the Nemotron Elastic framework; weights were inherited from the parent (compression lineage).
description_variants: BF16 was produced by compressing the parent NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 weights via the Nemotron Elastic framework; weights were inherited from the parent (compression lineage).

BF16 weights were initialized by compressing the parent NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 checkpoint via the Nemotron Elastic many-in-one framework, yielding a 3.97B-parameter hybrid Mamba-2/MLP/4-Attention model.

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-4B-BF16 was compressed from NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 (3.97B parameters, hybrid Mamba2-Transformer) using the Nemotron Elastic many-in-one framework; weights are inherited / initialized from the 9B-v2 parent.

Nemotron-3-Nano-4B-BF16 was compressed (weight initialization via parameter pruning/distillation) from NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 using the Nemotron Elastic framework (arXiv:2511.16664), yielding a 3.97B-parameter hybrid Mamba-Transformer.

anchor_list.explanation: Direct compression-from-parent statement establishing weight lineage.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16
edges.description: DeepSeek-V3 solved each generated MCQ with reasoning traces, sampled multiple completions, and applied majority voting to retain only consistent samples — producing the answer-and-rationale half of the multiple-choice subset of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1.
description_variants: DeepSeek-V3 solved each generated MCQ with reasoning traces, sampled multiple completions, and applied majority voting to retain only consistent samples — producing the answer-and-rationale half of the multiple-choice subset of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1.
DeepSeek-V3 (one of two generators) produced the Nemotron-Pretraining-Multiple-Choice synthetic corpus from MMLU Auxiliary Train prompts.
DeepSeek-V3 generated portions of the 6.7B-token Synthetic Tasks corpus from benchmark train-split seeds.
DeepSeek-V3 contributed to producing the Synthetic Social Sciences corpus.
DeepSeek-V3 contributed to producing the Synthetic Health Sciences corpus.
DeepSeek-V3 contributed to producing the Synthetic STEM corpus.
DeepSeek-V3 contributed to generating the Synthetic AGIEval corpus.
DeepSeek-V3 (root family node — base card cites the DeepSeek-V3 family without further pinning) was used as one of the generators for the Multiple-Choice synthetic sub-corpus.

Solutions/answers for the Synthetic MCQ corpus (Multiple-Choice subset of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1) were generated by DeepSeek-V3 with majority voting across multiple sampled completions; the resulting majority-voted answers anchor the supervised pretraining target.
DeepSeek-V3 produced reasoning traces by solving each generated MCQ with multiple sampled CoT traces; majority voting was applied to retain consistent answers for the synthetic-MCQ subset.
DeepSeek-V3 was used to generate solutions for the Synthetic MCQ subset using majority voting; that synthetic data was then ingested into Nemotron 3 Super pretraining.
DeepSeek-V3 generated synthetic multiple-choice content.
DeepSeek-V3 (DeepSeek v3) generated the synthetic tasks content.
DeepSeek-V3 generated synthetic social sciences content.
DeepSeek-V3 generated synthetic health sciences content.
DeepSeek-V3 generated STEM synthetic content.
DeepSeek-V3 generated synthetic AGIEval content.
DeepSeek-V3 generated solutions (with majority voting) for the Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 Multiple-Choice synthetic MCQ corpus that feeds Super3 Phase 1 pretraining.
DeepSeek-V3 produced answer/solution generations (with majority voting) for the Synthetic MCQ pretraining subset consumed by Nemotron 3 Super.

DeepSeek-V3 solved each generated MCQ with multi-seed sampling and provided the supporting knowledge / reasoning trace; majority voting across seeds retained the consistent samples for the MCQ dataset.

DeepSeek-V3 generated answer + reasoning trace solutions for each MCQ, with majority voting over multiple seeds used to retain consistent answers, in the Synthetic MCQ subset of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1.
DeepSeek-V3 generated reasoning-trace solutions to the synthesized MCQs (with majority voting across multiple seeds for answer reliability).
DeepSeek-V3 was used to generate solutions (with majority voting) for the Synthetic MCQ pretraining dataset.
DeepSeek-V3 was one of two generators for the 1609.2M-token Multiple-Choice subset of Specialized-v1.1, which builds MCQs on topics drawn from the MMLU auxiliary_train dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: SFT initializes from the Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 (1M-context) checkpoint and applies a two-stage SFT loss to produce the chat-stage model.
description_variants: SFT initializes from the Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 (1M-context) checkpoint and applies a two-stage SFT loss to produce the chat-stage model.
NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 (chat) initializes from the pre-trained Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 checkpoint and continues with SFT and RL post-training.
BF16 chat checkpoint is warm-started from the Base-BF16 pretrained model and proceeds through SFT (and subsequently RL) to produce the chat release.

RL stage continues from the post-SFT BF16 chat checkpoint (which itself derives from Base-BF16). Captured as trained_from for chat-vs-base lineage; the SFT->RL ordering is described in stages 2 and 3.

SFT initializes from the pretrained nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 checkpoint via Megatron-Bridge's finetune() entry point with role-based loss masking on assistant tokens only, and the resulting checkpoint is published as nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 (the post-trained release).
Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B (post-trained chat) is initialized from Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base after the 1M-context LC phase, and then progressed through SFT → RLVR → SWE-RL → RLHF → MTP healing.

The post-trained chat model (BF16) is initialized from the pretrained Base-BF16 checkpoint and fine-tuned via two-stage SFT followed by 6-stage RL. This trained_from edge represents the warm-start of the SFT stage from the pretraining output.

The Super3 SFT (Stage 1) checkpoint, which seeds all subsequent post-training stages culminating in the BF16 release, is initialized from the pretrained 500B-merge Base-BF16 checkpoint produced by Stage 0.
The post-trained Nemotron 3 Super Instruct continues training from the Nemotron 3 Super Base BF16 checkpoint through Stage 1 SFT and Stage 2 RL.
Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Chat is initialized from the Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base 1M-context checkpoint and continues with SFT, RLVR, SWE-RL, RLHF, and MTP-healing.

The post-trained Super 3 chat checkpoint (BF16) was initialized from the Stage-0 pretrained Base BF16 checkpoint (a 500B merge selected during the stable LR phase) before applying SFT, RL, and quantization.
BF16 release initialized from the Base-BF16 checkpoint and continued through SFT, multi-environment GRPO RL, and RLHF.

Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B (Instruct) post-training initializes from the 120B-A12B Base BF16 checkpoint and proceeds through SFT, RLVR, SWE-RL, RLHF, and MTP healing stages to produce the released BF16 Instruct model.
Final post-trained Nemotron 3 Super BF16 model is initialized from Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base BF16 and continues with 2-stage SFT, three RL stages (RLVR/SWE-RL/RLHF), and MTP healing.
SFT loads the pretrained Base BF16 checkpoint from Stage 0 and finetunes via Megatron-Bridge's finetune() entry point.

edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B (judge) selected the best of five GPT-OSS-120B candidate answers per financial-reasoning question on numerical accuracy, financial methodology, and logical soundness — included rather than rewrote responses.
description_variants: Qwen3-235B-A22B (judge) selected the best of five GPT-OSS-120B candidate answers per financial-reasoning question on numerical accuracy, financial methodology, and logical soundness — included rather than rewrote responses.

Qwen3-235B-A22B serves as the LM judge for equivalence and instruction-following decisions during RLVR rollouts (e.g., grading whether an open-ended answer matches the gold answer for math/IF tasks). Hosted as a NeMo Gym judge model on the same Ray cluster as the training job.
Qwen3-235B-A22B served as judge in GenSelect (Toshniwal et al., 2025), selecting the best of five GPT-OSS-120B answers per question for the Financial Reasoning SFT corpus.

Qwen3-235B-A22B (used as nl2bash_judge_model and equivalence judge in NeMo-Gym) is the LLM judge for equivalence and instruction-following scoring across all 3 RLVR sub-stages of Nemotron 3 Super; the served checkpoint is the Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8 quantization.
During RLVR, Qwen3-235B-A22B serves as the equivalence/instruction-following judge model: it scores rollouts for correctness equivalence and rubric-based IF compliance, gating reward signals.
Qwen3-235B-A22B serves as a judge during Multi-Environment RLVR for Nemotron 3 Super, scoring response equivalence and instruction-following compliance.
Qwen3-235B-A22B served as the GenSelect judge model selecting the best of five GPT-OSS-120B candidate answers per question based on numerical accuracy, financial methodology, and logical soundness.
Qwen3-235B-A22B served as the equivalence / instruction-following judge model in the RLVR (Stage 1.1-1.3) NeMo Gym, scoring rollout correctness for math/IF environments.

Qwen3-235B-A22B serves as an LLM judge during RLVR for equivalence and instruction-following reward computation, deciding which rollouts receive positive reward.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-FP8
edges.description: FP8 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model: per the Hopper GPU target, MoE/Mamba GEMMs and KV cache go to FP8; Mamba SSM cache in FP16; embedding/attention/output layers stay in BF16. Calibrated with 256 SFT samples at 65536 context.
description_variants: FP8 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model: per the Hopper GPU target, MoE/Mamba GEMMs and KV cache go to FP8; Mamba SSM cache in FP16; embedding/attention/output layers stay in BF16. Calibrated with 256 SFT samples at 65536 context.

FP8 checkpoint is produced by post-training quantization (PTQ) of the BF16 post-trained checkpoint via TensorRT-Model-Optimizer + Megatron-Bridge. No additional training, only quantization with 256 calibration samples drawn from the post-training SFT dataset.
nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-FP8 is post-training-quantized from the BF16 chat checkpoint nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16.

FP8 (W8A8) checkpoint is produced via PTQ from the post-trained BF16 checkpoint using Model-Optimizer; calibration uses 256 samples from the post-training SFT dataset (subject inherits all SFT/RL training data lineage from the BF16 base).
FP8 release derived via post-training quantization (Model-Optimizer PTQ) from the BF16 release; calibrated with 256 SFT samples.
The Nemotron 3 Super FP8 (W8A8) checkpoint is produced by post-training quantization of the aligned BF16 model with calibration on 256 SFT samples and Mamba state cache quantized to FP16.
Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B FP8 is produced by post-training quantization of the BF16 chat checkpoint via NVIDIA Model-Optimizer using 256 calibration samples from the SFT dataset.
The FP8 checkpoint was produced by post-training quantization of the BF16 aligned chat model (MoE GEMMs and Mamba GEMMs to FP8, FP8 KV cache, FP16 Mamba state cache; Mamba SSM kernel FP16).
FP8 is a quantized post-training release derived from the BF16 release (same training data and methodology, weights cast/quantized to FP8).
Nemotron 3 Super FP8 (W8A8) is produced by post-training quantization of the Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B BF16 Instruct checkpoint, calibrated on 256 SFT samples with 65,536 context length.
Nemotron 3 Super FP8 is produced from the BF16 post-trained model via post-training quantization (W8A8 with selective BF16/FP16 retention).
The FP8 quantized checkpoint is produced by post-training quantization of the BF16 post-trained Nemotron 3 Super model.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4
edges.description: NVFP4 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model with mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 sparse-expert GEMMs, FP8/BF16 attention/Mamba, BF16 embeddings/output) selected by Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize. Calibration: 512 samples at SL=4096 from Super SFT dataset. Achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to BF16.
description_variants: NVFP4 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model with mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 sparse-expert GEMMs, FP8/BF16 attention/Mamba, BF16 embeddings/output) selected by Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize. Calibration: 512 samples at SL=4096 from Super SFT dataset. Achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to BF16.
NVFP4 checkpoint is initialized from the BF16 post-trained checkpoint via PTQ + AutoQuantize mixed-precision NAS, then refined with QAD using BF16 as teacher.

QAD distillation refines the PTQ-quantized NVFP4 student using the BF16 post-trained checkpoint as teacher under a logit-based loss for 5B tokens. The same trained_from relation as PTQ — the student inherits weights from BF16 quantization and is fine-tuned to match BF16 logits.

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4 is post-training-quantized from the BF16 chat checkpoint nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16, using AutoQuantize-driven mixed-precision NVFP4/FP8/BF16 assignment.

NVFP4 (W4A4) checkpoint is initialized from the post-trained BF16 chat checkpoint via hybrid FP4 PTQ + AutoQuantize mixed-precision NAS, then refined via QAD (BF16 teacher → NVFP4 student) on a 5B-token SFT+RL on-policy 60:40 blend.

NVFP4 release derived from BF16 release via hybrid PTQ + AutoQuantize NAS + Quantization-Aware Distillation with the BF16 model as teacher (5B-token training budget; 60:40 SFT+RL on-policy rollouts data blend).

Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B NVFP4 is produced by hybrid mixed-precision PTQ of the BF16 chat checkpoint via NVIDIA Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize, with 512 calibration samples drawn from the SFT dataset.
The NVFP4 checkpoint was produced by hybrid PTQ + AutoQuantize NAS + QAD distillation from the BF16 chat checkpoint, with the BF16 checkpoint serving as both PTQ source and QAD teacher.
NVFP4 is a quantized post-training release derived from the BF16 release (same training data and methodology, weights quantized to NVFP4).

Nemotron 3 Super NVFP4 (W4A4) is produced by mixed-precision PTQ from the BF16 Instruct checkpoint, achieving 99.8% median accuracy relative to the BF16 baseline while retaining near-FP4 inference performance on Blackwell.
Nemotron 3 Super NVFP4 is produced from the BF16 post-trained model via Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize-driven mixed-precision quantization (mostly NVFP4 + selective FP8/BF16).
NVFP4 student is initialised from the BF16 post-trained Nemotron 3 Super checkpoint via PTQ; the BF16 model is also the QAD teacher.

SWE-bench_Verified used as a training data source (separate from its eval use).
SWE 2 RL rollouts use SWE-Bench Verified Docker images as a third sandbox source for the OpenHands-loop binary-reward training.

SWE-Bench Verified used as RL environment in Stage 2.2 SWE-RL: each rollout launches an isolated container with the target repo, OpenHands agent loop produces a code patch (up to 200 turns), ground-truth tests give binary reward.
SWE2 uses SWE-bench Verified environments (Docker images converted to .sif) for the full SWE-bench RL training with binary test-pass reward.

SWE 2 rollouts run inside SWE-Bench Verified container images (along with R2E-Gym and SWE-Gym), with each task's ground-truth tests providing the binary RL reward — the verified task instances enter the training stream as RL prompts.
Post-training on SWE-bench_Verified data.

SWE-RL Stage 2.2 uses SWE-Bench Verified container images (alongside R2E-Gym and SWE-Gym) as the environment basis for RL training of Super 120B-A12B Instruct, with Apptainer sandboxes and OpenHands agent loops.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4
edges.description: QAD distillation data includes RL on-policy rollouts (40% of the 60:40 blend) generated by the BF16 post-trained model itself, used to align student logits with teacher behavior on production-distribution prompts.
description_variants: QAD distillation data includes RL on-policy rollouts (40% of the 60:40 blend) generated by the BF16 post-trained model itself, used to align student logits with teacher behavior on production-distribution prompts.

FP4 QAD distillation: the BF16 checkpoint serves as the teacher whose logits supervise the NVFP4 student over 5B tokens of SFT + RL on-policy rollouts. Treat as generated_by because the teacher's logit/hidden distributions are the training target for the student.
QAD distillation generates logit/hidden targets from the BF16 teacher to supervise the NVFP4 student over 5B tokens.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base
edges.description: Phi-4 was the lightweight LLM used in the Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 construction pipeline to clean Common-Crawl math content (boilerplate removal, LaTeX standardization), producing the 206.2B math tokens consumed by nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base.
description_variants: Phi-4 was the lightweight LLM used in the Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 construction pipeline to clean Common-Crawl math content (boilerplate removal, LaTeX standardization), producing the 206.2B math tokens consumed by nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base.
Phi-4 (14B parameters) rewrote raw HTML pages of the math corpus to remove boilerplate, standardize notation into LaTeX, and correct inconsistencies during construction of Nemotron-CC-Math-v1.

Within Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 (used by nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base), microsoft/phi-4 (14B) was used as a lightweight LLM cleaner: it removed boilerplate, standardized math expressions into LaTeX, and improved formatting of Lynx-rendered Common Crawl pages. Phi-4 transformed existing HTML/text content rather than generating it from scratch. Token volume credited to Phi-4 in the master table is 206.2B.

microsoft/phi-4 served as the LLM in the Lynx + LLM pipeline that transformed Common Crawl HTML into LaTeX-standardized math text for Nemotron-CC-Math-v1, contributing 206.2B tokens of training content for Nemotron Nano 2.

Phi-4 (14B parameters) rewrote raw lynx-rendered math web HTML to remove boilerplate, standardize math notation into LaTeX, and correct inconsistencies, producing the cleaned text that became Nemotron-CC-Math.

Phi-4 transformed Nemotron-CC-Math-4+ chunks (5K-token chunks) into structured math dialogues using seven prompt templates (Teacher-Student, Debate, Interview, ...), producing the regenerated Nemotron-MIND corpus.
Phi-4 (14B) rewrote text in nvidia/Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 that was part of the Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base pretraining mixture.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base
edges.description: Qwen3-30B-A3B was used to rephrase Common Crawl content into 879.1B synthetic English tokens for Nemotron-CC-v2, including diverse-QA generation and translation; this rephrased content was consumed by nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base.
description_variants: Qwen3-30B-A3B was used to rephrase Common Crawl content into 879.1B synthetic English tokens for Nemotron-CC-v2, including diverse-QA generation and translation; this rephrased content was consumed by nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base.
Qwen3-30B-A3B performed synthetic rephrasing of English Common-Crawl content for Nemotron-CC-v2 in this release (replacing the previously used Mistral Nemo 12B).

Qwen3-30B-A3B was used to translate the English Diverse QA into 15 target languages (yielding the Translated-Diverse-QA subset of Nemotron-CC-v2) and to translate a subset of the GSM8K augmentation data; outputs were post-processed with a localized 'The answer is …' template.
Qwen3-30B-A3B synthetically rephrased Common Crawl content for Nemotron-CC-v2, contributing 879.1B rephrased tokens to the Nemotron Nano 2 pretraining mix.

Qwen3-30B-A3B was the synthetic rephraser used to rewrite raw Common Crawl English web text into higher-quality 'syn-crawl-high' pretraining data; it replaced Mistral-NeMo-12B-Instruct used in the original Nemotron-CC pipeline.

Qwen3-30B-A3B translated English Diverse-QA into 15 languages and translated a subset of GSM8K-augmentation solutions into the same 15 languages with appended conclusion sentence ('La respuesta es...', 'Die Antwort lautet...', ...). Source content originated elsewhere; Qwen3-30B-A3B only translated.

Qwen3-30B-A3B generated 4.2B tokens of synthetic fundamental-reasoning SFT-style pretraining data (questions plus solutions) seeded from LSAT, LogiQA, and AQuA-RAT.

Qwen3-30B-A3B is the new primary rephraser and Diverse-QA translator (into 15 languages) for Nemotron-CC-v2 synthetic data — 879.1B tokens of synthetic content in Nemotron-CC-v2 are attributed to it. Replaces Mistral-Nemo-12B-Instruct as the default rephraser.

Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct generated long-context QA pairs from 1,024-token chunks (10% sampled) of >32k-token academic documents; QA pairs appended to original document as long-context training samples for Phase LC.

edges.description: MMLU-Pro is one of 12 benchmarks used to compare best-of-3 offline-merged checkpoints vs the trained checkpoint over the 25T-token Nemotron 3 Super pretraining run (Figure 11 / Figure 17).
description_variants: MMLU-Pro is one of 12 benchmarks used to compare best-of-3 offline-merged checkpoints vs the trained checkpoint over the 25T-token Nemotron 3 Super pretraining run (Figure 11 / Figure 17).
MMLU Pro 5 Shot CoT is one of the eight benchmarks tracked in the MXFP8-promotion healing ablation; switching to MXFP8 for the final 1T tokens yielded no sustained downstream improvement.

Used in the offline checkpoint-merging ablation (Section 2.5, Figure 11 + Appendix A Figure 17) comparing merged-versus-trained checkpoints for Super 120B-A12B Base across 12 benchmarks; reported per-benchmark on MMLU-Pro.

Used in the NVFP4-to-MXFP8 precision-promotion ablation (Figure 9, Section 2.2) measuring downstream task accuracy improvement when promoting from NVFP4 to MXFP8 at 19T tokens — reported on MMLU Pro, 5 Shot CoT.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.
description_variants: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.

DR-Tulu-SFT-8B continues from the Qwen/Qwen3-8B base checkpoint via full fine-tuning (finetuning_type: full) with the qwen3 chat template; this is the warm start for the SFT stage that precedes the RL stage.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B

edges.subject: nvidia/OpenMath-Nemotron-7B
edges.description: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct performed problem extraction and summary rewriting for the OpenMathReasoning data consumed by OpenMath-Nemotron-7B.
description_variants: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct performed problem extraction and summary rewriting for the OpenMathReasoning data consumed by OpenMath-Nemotron-7B.

Problems consumed by OpenMath-Nemotron-7B (via OpenMathReasoning) were preprocessed (extraction, classification, answer extraction, proof conversion, decontamination) by Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct preprocessed AoPS forum posts into clean problem statements (extract_problems / classify_problems / extract_answers / convert_proofs stages) and generated replacement summaries for reasoning solutions, producing the problem and summary content nvidia/OpenMath-Nemotron-7B trains on.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct rewrote DeepSeek-R1's terse native summaries into 2048-token summaries for the OpenMathReasoning CoT solutions and regenerated all GenSelect comparison summaries; these rewrites were ingested by OpenMath-Nemotron-7B during SFT.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct rewrote the AoPS forum content into clean problems (extract-problems prompt), converted proof problems into answer-bearing form, extracted answers, and regenerated all OpenMathReasoning solution summaries used in SFT.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct served as the default LLM judge for problem extraction, classification (proof / multiple-choice / binary / valid), answer-equivalence checks, and the novel/significance/trivial classifier on code blocks for TIR filtering — deciding inclusion of solutions in the OpenMathReasoning data used to SFT OpenMath-Nemotron-7B.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct served as the LLM judge that decided inclusion: classifying problems (proof / MCQ / binary / invalid), judging answer correctness, judging summary faithfulness, classifying TIR-fragment novelty/significance, and performing benchmark decontamination against MATH/AIME/AMC/etc.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base
edges.description: Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base is pretrained using DeepSeek-V3's FP8 training recipe (E4M3, 128x128 weight blocks, 1x128 activation tiles), with a small modification (BF16 first/last four linear layers, all-gather in E4M3 instead of native E4M3 weights).
description_variants: Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base is pretrained using DeepSeek-V3's FP8 training recipe (E4M3, 128x128 weight blocks, 1x128 activation tiles), with a small modification (BF16 first/last four linear layers, all-gather in E4M3 instead of native E4M3 weights).

Pretraining of Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base used DeepSeek-V3's FP8 training recipe (E4M3 for tensors, 128x128 quant blocks for weights, 1x128 tiles for activations) for the entirety of the run, with first/last four linear layers in BF16 as in Nemotron-H.

Pretraining used DeepSeek-V3's FP8 recipe (E4M3 tensors, 128x128 weight quantization blocks, 1x128 activation tiles), with native E4M3 weight storage for the distributed-optimizer parameter all-gather; first/last four linear layers kept in BF16 (as in Nemotron-H).

anchor_list.excerpt: We used DeepSeek's FP8 training recipe for the entirety of the pretraining run (DeepSeek-AI, 2025b). Specifically, we used E4M3 for all tensors, 128x128 quantization blocks for weights, and 1x128 tiles for the activations.

anchor_list.explanation: Paper documents the regenerated 73B-token MIND corpus consumed in 12B-v2-Base pretraining.

edges.description: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 trained on nvidia/Nemotron-MIND (the regenerated Nemotron-MIND-V1 release, 73B tokens, 88.73M documents) which the team rebuilt using the high-quality Nemotron-CC-Math-4plus subset; the regeneration yielded consistent gains over the original Nemotron-MIND across MMLU/MMLU-STEM/MMLU-Pro/Math-500/GSM8K/Code.

anchor_list.explanation: Card describes the rebuilt MIND that supports the Nemotron Nano 2 family; consumer is in that family.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 derives from the 12B base via the alignment pipeline applied first on 12B (SFT 1/2/3 → DPO → GRPO → RLHF → Merge) and then carried into the 9B reasoning model through pruning and distillation.
description_variants: Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 derives from the 12B base via the alignment pipeline applied first on 12B (SFT 1/2/3 → DPO → GRPO → RLHF → Merge) and then carried into the 9B reasoning model through pruning and distillation.

Reasoning-aligned 9B-v2 inherits its weights from the 12B-v2-Base teacher via the depth+width pruning pipeline (Section 4.3) and post-pruning recovery (DPO+GRPO+KD+RLHF+Merge); the pruned base 9B-v2-Base sits between them in the lineage.

Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 inherits its weights from the aligned 12B (which was initialized from the 12B-v2-Base) via the alignment chain (SFT 1/2/3 -> DPO branch / RLHF+GRPO branch -> Model Merge -> Minitron pruning + distillation to 9B). The base-model layer state in 9B-v2 traces directly to the 12B-v2-Base.

Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 weights derive from the merged 12B aligned checkpoint, which is initialized from the 12B-v2-Base; pruning + KD + DPO + GRPO + RLHF + checkpoint interpolation produce the final 9B-v2.

anchor_list.excerpt: We aligned Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base using several stages of SFT, GRPO, DPO, and RLHF before using the Minitron compression via pruning and distillation strategy to produce the final model.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms 9B-v2 inherits from 12B-v2-Base via alignment + compression.

anchor_list.excerpt: We have open-sourced Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 along with its corresponding sibling Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2-Base and parent Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base models
anchor_list.explanation: States 12B-v2-Base is the parent of 9B-v2.

anchor_list.excerpt: After aligning Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base, we employ the Minitron strategy to compress and distill the model... we will present the alignment process we followed to convert the base checkpoint into an aligned 12B checkpoint.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper documents the chain from 12B-v2-Base through alignment to 9B-v2.

anchor_list.excerpt: We create Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 by first pre-training a 12-billion-parameter model (Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base)... we continue training Candidate 2 in an extended phase, as detailed below, to yield the final Nano 2 reasoning and base models.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper traces 9B-v2 weights to 12B-v2-Base via the alignment-then-distillation chain.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: Depth-pruning candidates (52/54/56-layer variants distilled from the 12B base via KD with 6B tokens; Table 9) and width-pruning candidates (Table 10) were the team's own architecture-search ablations leading to the Nano-2 56-layer Candidate 2 architecture.

anchor_list.excerpt: We compare the accuracy of three depth-pruned candidates obtained from the 12B model with 52, 54 and 56 layers.
anchor_list.explanation: Self-ablation over the 12B teacher to pick the 9B-v2 architecture.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: The LIMO 32B reasoning model is initialized from Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct and continues training via full-parameter SFT for 15 epochs at lr 5.0e-6 (cosine decay, no warmup), batch size 64, with sequences up to 16,384 tokens.
description_variants: The LIMO 32B reasoning model is initialized from Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct and continues training via full-parameter SFT for 15 epochs at lr 5.0e-6 (cosine decay, no warmup), batch size 64, with sequences up to 16,384 tokens.
GAIR/LIMO (32B reasoning model) is initialized from Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct and continues with full-parameter SFT.

anchor_list.excerpt: We fine-tune Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct using supervised fine-tuning on our LIMO dataset.
anchor_list.explanation: Explicitly identifies Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the warm-start base for the LIMO SFT run.

anchor_list.excerpt: Following LIMO principles, we carefully construct the LIMO dataset and fine-tune Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct through simple SFT.
anchor_list.explanation: Reiterates Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the LIMO base checkpoint.

anchor_list.excerpt: | LIMO | Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct  | 32B | ... Our model is fine-tuned on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct
anchor_list.explanation: Names Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the LIMO backbone fine-tuned to produce GAIR/LIMO.

model_name_or_path: Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct
stage: sft

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B served as the fine-grained difficulty filter: 32 solution attempts per remaining problem; only problems with low empirical success rate (1-3/32) were retained, yielding the 2,125-problem LIMO-Pool.

anchor_list.excerpt: we subjected the filtered problems to a more rigorous evaluation using a stronger reasoning model, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B (Guo et al., 2025). For each remaining problem, we sampled 32 solution attempts and used the empirical success rate as a difficulty indicator. Problems that were successfully solved in only 1-3 out of 32 attempts were retained
anchor_list.explanation: Documents DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B as the second-stage difficulty filter for LIMO question selection.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 was one of three reasoning-chain generators for LIMO: it sampled multiple candidate solutions per filtered question; the highest-scoring chain per problem (after rule-based quality scoring) was retained as the SFT solution.

anchor_list.excerpt: we employed three state-of-the-art reasoning models—DeepSeek R1, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B (Guo et al., 2025), and QwQ-32B (Team, 2025b)—sampling multiple solutions from each
anchor_list.explanation: Names DeepSeek-R1 as a candidate-solution generator whose outputs end up in the LIMO SFT corpus.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B was both used to filter LIMO questions for difficulty and used as one of three reasoning-chain generators; its sampled solutions competed in the rule-based scoring round before the top 800 pairs were retained.

anchor_list.excerpt: we employed three state-of-the-art reasoning models—DeepSeek R1, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B (Guo et al., 2025), and QwQ-32B (Team, 2025b)—sampling multiple solutions from each to generate diverse reasoning approaches.
anchor_list.explanation: Names DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B as one of the three reasoning-chain generators for LIMO.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: QwQ-32B (Team 2025b release) was one of three reasoning-chain generators for LIMO: it sampled multiple candidate solutions per filtered question; surviving solutions were ranked by rule-based quality scoring and the top 800 pairs entered the LIMO SFT corpus.

anchor_list.excerpt: we employed three state-of-the-art reasoning models—DeepSeek R1, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B (Guo et al., 2025), and QwQ-32B (Team, 2025b)
anchor_list.explanation: Names QwQ-32B as one of the three reasoning-chain generators for LIMO; references Team 2025b which is the QwQ-32B release blog post.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct was the largest backbone in the LIMO RQ4 model-size ablation: SFT on LIMO 800 reaches 68.3% AIME24, only marginally above the 32B run, suggesting saturation in fine-tuning gains at very large scales.

anchor_list.excerpt: rising from 2.5 for the 3B model to 68.3 for the 72B model. ... The difference in performance between the 32B and 72B models is marginal on MATH500 (95.6% vs. 94.8%) and slightly less pronounced on AIME24 (63.3% vs. 68.3%)

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B
edges.description: The 7.5M natural language solutions in Nemotron-Math-v2 used to fine-tune Qwen3-30B-A3B were generated by gpt-oss-120b in three reasoning regimes (high/medium/low), with and without Python TIR.
description_variants: The 7.5M natural language solutions in Nemotron-Math-v2 used to fine-tune Qwen3-30B-A3B were generated by gpt-oss-120b in three reasoning regimes (high/medium/low), with and without Python TIR.

All 7.5M long-form reasoning traces in the Nemotron-Math training data consumed by Qwen3-30B-A3B were generated by gpt-oss-120b under high/medium/low reasoning modes, both with and without Python tool-integrated reasoning (16 solutions per problem: 8 Python-TIR + 8 without).
gpt-oss-120b generated all 7.5M natural-language solutions in Nemotron-Math-v2 (8 with-Python and 8 no-Python solutions per problem, in three reasoning_effort regimes) that Qwen3-30B-A3B trains on.

anchor_list.excerpt: 7.5M natural language solutions generated by gpt-oss-120b
anchor_list.explanation: All SFT solutions consumed by Qwen3-30B-A3B's SFT come from gpt-oss-120b.

anchor_list.excerpt: We use gpt-oss-120b to generate solutions in three modes (high, medium, and low), both with and without Python Tool Integrated Reasoning (TIR).
anchor_list.explanation: The solution generation step is performed by gpt-oss-120b.

anchor_list.excerpt: Based on the prepared problem set, we prompt the gpt-oss-120b model to generate solutions in three reasoning modes, high, medium, and low, under both Python TIR and without Python TIR settings, yielding six configurations in total.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents gpt-oss-120b as the sole generator of every Nemotron-Math reasoning trace.

anchor_list.excerpt: We used Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct to preprocess problems, and gpt-oss-120b generate solutions.
anchor_list.explanation: Index names gpt-oss-120b as the solution generator for Nemotron-Math-v2.

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B
edges.description: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct is used to preprocess (extract/classify/decontaminate) the AoPS and StackExchange-Math problems that ultimately become Qwen3-30B-A3B's training set.
description_variants: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct is used to preprocess (extract/classify/decontaminate) the AoPS and StackExchange-Math problems that ultimately become Qwen3-30B-A3B's training set.
Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct serves as the LLM-as-a-judge for hle-math evaluation of the SFT-trained Qwen3-30B-A3B.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct decided inclusion of training items in two ways: (i) acted as an LLM-as-a-judge comparing extracted answers to gpt-oss-120b solutions to validate examples, and (ii) served as the proof-style classifier filtering out theorem-proving items from the StackExchange-Math source.

Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct served as the LLM judge in the Nemotron-Math-v2 judge_answers stage that compared each gpt-oss-120b solution's final answer to the expected answer for filtering, before SFT data was prepared for Qwen3-30B-A3B.

anchor_list.excerpt: We used Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct to preprocess problems
anchor_list.explanation: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct performs problem-extraction, classification, and decontamination filters on the upstream forum data.

anchor_list.excerpt: We use the Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct model as the judge ... judge_model='/workspace/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct'
anchor_list.explanation: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct judges hle-math outputs of the SFT model.

anchor_list.excerpt: we follow Moshkov et al. [15] and use Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct [10] as an LLM-as-a-judge, comparing the expected answers with the final answers extracted from the generated solutions.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the answer judge that decides inclusion of training items.

anchor_list.excerpt: Proof-style questions are filtered following the procedure described in Moshkov et al. [15], which employs the Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct model as a classifier.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the proof-style classifier filtering the StackExchange-Math problem pool.

anchor_list.excerpt: This step is performed using the judge_answers stage. ... we run judge_answers again to judge each model-generated solution's final answer against the finalized expected answer.
anchor_list.explanation: Dataset doc describes the judge_answers stage; the Nemotron-Math-v2 evaluation doc and OMR config templates use Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as judge by default.
anchor_list.position: ### Prepare Expected Answers / Re-judge against the finalized expected answer

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-32B
edges.description: The supervised fine-tuning data consumed by Qwen3-32B (yielding a fine-tuned NoC reasoning variant) is generated by gpt-oss-120b in two phases: Phase 1 produces structured incident resolution procedures via formatting_prompt.yaml; Phase 2 injects detailed reasoning traces via shortened_prompt_reasoning.yaml. The output is compiled into outputs/sft-data-incidence.jsonl which is then used by `ns nemo_rl sft` with --hf_model=Qwen/Qwen3-32B.

DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B was used as a difficulty estimator scoring problems 0-16; problems with difficulty 0 or 16 specific to this size-matched 7B variant were excluded from 7B-Preview's training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: difficulty = example['extra_info']['model_difficulty'].get('DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B') ... if difficulty < 1 or difficulty > 15: return False
anchor_list.explanation: The 7B preprocessing filter explicitly keys on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B difficulty labels.

anchor_list.excerpt: we filtered problems based on their difficulty levels (0-16) ... relative to specific model variants (DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-{1.5,7,32}B). For each model variant, we excluded problems with difficulty values of 0 and 16 specific to that model from its training data.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B as the difficulty estimator whose 0/16 scores filtered the size-matched 7B model's training data.

edges.subject: Skywork/Skywork-OR1-7B-Preview

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen2.5-Math-7B
edges.description: The persona-guided synthetic math pool's problems are generated by 5-shot prompting Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct conditioned on randomly-sampled personas; this pool is then used to train Qwen2.5-Math-7B.
description_variants: The persona-guided synthetic math pool's problems are generated by 5-shot prompting Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct conditioned on randomly-sampled personas; this pool is then used to train Qwen2.5-Math-7B.
The vanilla-few-shot synthetic math pool's problems are generated by 5-shot prompting Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct without persona conditioning.

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's weights are initialized from Qwen3-32B and continued via expert-iteration plus RL post-training with scaffolded data synthesis, verifier-guided self-correction, and checkpoint averaging.
description_variants: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's weights are initialized from Qwen3-32B and continued via expert-iteration plus RL post-training with scaffolded data synthesis, verifier-guided self-correction, and checkpoint averaging.
Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is fine-tuned from Qwen3-32B as the base model for the V2 prover series.

anchor_list.explanation: HF model card frontmatter standardly declares the base/init checkpoint of a derived model; here it names Qwen/Qwen3-32B as the base for the 32B Goedel prover.

anchor_list.excerpt: We employ S1 to conduct SFT on both Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-32B, resulting in fine-tuned models SFT-S1 for both the 8B and 32B variants.
anchor_list.explanation: States Qwen3-32B is the base checkpoint that the Goedel-Prover-V2 32B SFT pipeline starts from.

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B drives informal-based scaffolded data synthesis: for each problem it produces simpler/sub-problems (when unsolved) or harder variants (when already solved), plus an attempted natural-language solution used as context. These generated informal problems are then formalized and added to S3.

anchor_list.excerpt: For a given problem, we prompt an LLM (Qwen3-32B) to generate simpler/sub-problems if it is unsolved, or harder variants if it is already solved.
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies Qwen3-32B as the generator of the scaffolded informal problems that, after formalization, become training data for the 32B prover.
anchor_list.position: Section 2.2 Informal-based scaffolded data synthesis, page 4

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B serves as the LLM judge that filters generated formal statements for correctness and difficulty (4 majority-vote inferences per formalization) in the negation-and-difficulty filtering stage of the scaffolded data synthesis pipeline used to build S3.

anchor_list.excerpt: For each formalization, we query Qwen3-32B for 4 times, where each inference judges the correctness and the simplicity simultaneously. The final correctness is judged by strict majority voting among the 4 judges.
anchor_list.explanation: Appendix D explicitly documents Qwen3-32B as the inclusion-decision filter for synthesized formal statements feeding the 32B prover's S3 training data.
anchor_list.position: Appendix D, page 23, item 3 Negation and difficulty filtering

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Qwen3-8B is the formalization-quality checker (as the trained Goedel-Formalizer-V2-8B is queried 3 times via majority voting) within the formalization-and-quality-checking stage; this gates which scaffolded formal statements enter the 32B prover's training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: For each generated informal statement, we call the trained formalizer to formalize the problem twice. Then, we then query Qwen3-8B for 3 times for each formalization, and decide if the formalization is aligned with the informal statement using majority voting.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen3-8B as a quality-alignment filter on formalizations entering the 32B prover's scaffolded training data.
anchor_list.position: Appendix D, page 23, item 2 Formalization and quality checking
